Facility Engineering · Pharmaceutical

Pharmaceutical HVAC & BMS Reliability Support

Technical portfolio case study on HVAC/BMS troubleshooting, AHU performance follow-up, vendor coordination, and reliability monitoring in a pharmaceutical facility environment.

HVAC / BMS / GMP Support 2026 PT Daewoong Pharmaceutical Indonesia
hvac / bms

Case study

Overview Challenge Role Impact Details

BMS

data review and monitoring

AHU/VRF

system troubleshooting scope

GMP

facility engineering context

Problem / engineering challenge

Pharmaceutical facility HVAC operation requires stable temperature, reliable equipment response, disciplined troubleshooting, and careful coordination because air handling and room condition stability directly affect operational readiness.

My role

Supported engineering follow-up through BAS/BMS review, operational data checking, field coordination, vendor communication, issue documentation, and technical reporting.

Outcome / impact

Improved technical visibility of HVAC operation and supported more structured troubleshooting, follow-up, and management reporting for facility reliability.

Tools, systems, and references

Azbil savic-net G5BMS/BASLG Multi V VRFAHUExcelVendor coordinationTechnical reporting

Context

In a pharmaceutical facility, HVAC performance is not only a comfort issue. It is part of the facility operation ecosystem where temperature stability, equipment readiness, and technical documentation matter.

The portfolio case focuses on how engineering follow-up was structured around HVAC/BMS monitoring, AHU/VRF troubleshooting, and vendor coordination.

Engineering Challenge

The challenge was to move from reactive complaint handling toward a more structured technical investigation process: checking available BMS data, comparing operating conditions, coordinating with vendors, and documenting the technical status clearly.

  • Identify abnormal HVAC operating behavior from available system data.
  • Support field checks while considering limited access, tool availability, and production constraints.
  • Coordinate with service vendors for deeper inspection and corrective actions.
  • Translate technical findings into concise updates for engineering and management.

Approach

The work was approached by combining BMS/BAS observation, field communication, equipment status review, vendor follow-up, and concise technical reporting. This is important because HVAC issues often require both data interpretation and practical site coordination.

  • Reviewed HVAC-related operating signals and system behavior from BMS/BAS interface.
  • Supported follow-up on AHU/VRF operating condition and issue chronology.
  • Coordinated technical checks with service vendors and internal stakeholders.
  • Documented issue timeline, suspected causes, corrective actions, and follow-up items.

Impact

The impact was not only equipment recovery, but improved engineering visibility. The team gained a clearer issue chronology, better vendor communication, and a more disciplined basis for future HVAC reliability follow-up.
